fields.description:
Problem Statement: It's possible to leave your house and forget to turn down the temperature. This is costly and wasteful use of energy.

 

The solution should be able to be controlled remotely.
fields.name: Remote Control

fields.description:
Problem Statement: When encountering a foreign thermostat, it can be confusing to understand how to operate it. This leads to a poor user experience and adoption.

 

The solution shall be easy to operate and learn to use.
fields.name: Ease of Use

fields.description:
Problem Statement: Regardless of how well scheduling software is designed, it can still be confusing and unapproachable to first time users.

 

The system shall provide recommended schedules based on observed usage and indoor conditions.
fields.name: Intelligent Scheduling

fields.description:
Problem Statement: Thermostats have a tendency to be difficult to read from a great distance. It's desirable to be able to read the current temperature from across a room.

 

The solution should have a large enough display to read from across a large room.
fields.name: Clear Display

fields.description: As the homeowner, I'd like to be able to adjust the temperature of the thermostat while I'm on vacation, so that I can avoid expensive energy use while I'm away.
fields.name: Homeowner on Vacation
fields.postconditions$44: The temperature of the thermostat is set at the desired level.
fields.preconditions$44: User of the Thermostat is on vacation

fields.description: Validate that the thermostat on/off setting can be controlled when the user is not in the house.
fields.name: On/Off Remote Adjustment

fields.description: Validate that the thermostat temperature setting can be controlled when the user is not in the house.
fields.name: Temperature Remote Adjustment
Displayed Temperature can be seen from 3 meters away

testCaseSteps.action: View from 3 meters

fields.description: The system shall include wifi functionality to enable remote control of the thermostat.
fields.name: Wifi-Enabled

fields.description: The system shall be built on top of a rotating base to allow for adjustment of temperature by turning clockwise (increase in temp) and counter-clockwise (decrease in temp).
fields.name: Rotating Base

fields.description: The software solution shall allow for weekly scheduling of on/off and temperature ranges.
fields.name: Manual Scheduling

fields.description: The system shall recommend scheduling options based on normal usage and ongoing indoor climate conditions.
fields.name: Intelligent Scheduling

fields.description: The system shall support the simultaneous display of 3 characters
fields.name: Three Digit Display

fields.description: The system shall monitor ambient temperature and relative humidity
fields.name: Climate Monitoring

fields.description: The system shall monitor user interactions with temperature adjustments, heating/cooling power functions, and schedule changes.
fields.name: User Interaction Monitoring

fields.description: The thermostat shall learn the user's temperature preferences and develop an automatic schedule.
fields.name: Smart Scheduling
Cylindrical Base Design
Circuit Design

testCaseSteps.action: Rotate Clockwise 5 degrees
Rotate Counter-clockwise 5 degrees

fields.description: The system shall support the simultaneous display of 3 characters via mini 8X8 LED matrices.
fields.name: Mini 8x8 LED Matrix
LED Matrix Package Dimension
Typical Electrical / Optical Curves
Peak Forward Current Per Dot (1/10 Duty Cycle, 0.1ms Pulse Width)

fields.description:
The system shall leverage an I2C Backpack that permits the connection of all 3 mini 8x8 LED matrices.

 

  • This board/chip shall use I2C 7-bit address between 0x70-0x77, selectable with jumpers

 

Backpack Dimensions:

  • 20mm x 28mm x 4mm / 0.8" x 1.1" x 0.2"
  • Mounting Holes: 12mm x 22mm / 0.5" x 0.9"
fields.name: I2C Backpack

fields.description: Test the LED output for each cell.
fields.name: LED Display is Readable

fields.description: Temperature shall be sensed within an accuracy of .2 degrees Fahrenheit.
fields.name: Sensor Chip

fields.description: The sensor shall also detect humidity, and adjust the temperature appropriately taking into account perceived temperature.
fields.name: Humidity Sensing
I2C Timing Diagram
SOIC-8 SM Typical Application Circuits
SOIC-8 SMD Mounting Dimensions, PCB Landing Pattern and Pinout

fields.description: The sensor shall detect motion at a 170 degree range and 15 feet distance.
fields.name: Motion Sensor Chip

fields.description: using the mobile app GPS, automatically set the thermostat to away when you leave your home
fields.name: Auto Away detection

fields.description: Automatically detect when the user arrives at home using the mobile phone GPS. Set the thermostat to home when this is detected
fields.name: Arrive at home detection

fields.description: User must be able to set a max and min temperature
fields.name: Set max/min tempeture

fields.description: User should be able to torn on/off the heat and cooling of their thermostat remotely from the app
fields.name: Set Heat/Cool options

fields.description: User should be able to set a max/min, and away/home options daily from a scheduling screen
fields.name: Daily scheduling

fields.description: User should be able to see a calendar of their energy usage history.
fields.name: Energy history

fields.description: User should be able to set multiple zones to manage if they have multiple smart thermostats.
fields.name: Multiple zones

fields.description:
As a User
I can Turn on auto away
So that Smart thermostat automatically sets away settings when I leave my house
fields.name: Ability to turn on/off auto away

fields.description:
As a User
I can drag my finger on graphical thermostat display to set max/min temperature
So that I can override scheduled min/max.
fields.name: Set manual max/min temperature

fields.description:
As a User
I can turn the heat on and off
So that the heating element of my thermostat will not come on under any conditions
fields.name: Turn heater off/on

fields.description:
As a user
I can set cooling to off/on
So that I can turn off the cooling element of my thermostat so that the A/C will not come on under any conditions
fields.name: Turn A/C on/off

fields.description:
As a User
I can press the schedule button to view/edit my thermostat schedule
So that I can modify my schedule remotely from my mobile app
fields.name: open scheduling calendar

fields.description:
As a User
I can Select the edit button from my schedule view
So that I can modify my thermostat schedule
fields.name: Enter Edit mode of thermostat schedule

fields.description:
As a User
I can set min/max temperatures for each day of the week, hour by hour
So that my thermostat will adhere to the temperatures on a daily basis that I want it to
fields.name: Set daily min/max temperature for all 7 days of the week, hour by hour

fields.description:
As a User
I can When in away mode, I can turn on conservation mode
So that My thermostat will use minimal energy why I am not at home.

 

NOTE: There should be a warning that users home may not be heated to their liking when they arrive at home with this setting enabled.
fields.name: Conservation mode

fields.description:
As a User
I can select a history button from the home screen
So that I can see my energy consumption over the last week.
fields.name: History Button

fields.description:
As a User
I can when in history mode, I can see a calendar representation of my energy consumption over the last 7 days
So that I can see how conservative I am and make adjustments if needed
fields.name: View energy consumption history

fields.description:
As a User
I can select 'compare'
So that I can see a side by side comparison of my energy consumption compared to the average consumption from other smart thermostats in my area.
fields.name: Compare window

testCaseSteps.action: Verify that app status is set to 'home'
Turn on auto-away
Drive 1 mile away from location where unit is installed
Verify that app automatically turns status to 'away'
have another person verify that the installed unit is now set to 'away'
Verify that app status is set to 'away' and you are at least 1 mile away from installed device
Turn on auto-away
Drive to installed unit location
Verify that app automatically turns status to 'home'
verify that the installed unit is now set to 'home'
stand in front of installed unit
using your app, manually drag the min and max temperature markers from the home screen
verify that display on device lights up with the new min/max settings that you have chosen.
If temperature is outside of range that you chose, verify that the heat or A/C comes on as expected
from your app press the heat/cool button
turn heat off
turn temperature on device up above current tempurature
verify that the heat does not come on
turn heat option back on
verify that heat comes on
from your app press the heat/cool button
turn cool off
turn temperature on device up below current tempurature
verify that the A/C does not come on
turn cool option back on
verify that A/C comes on
From home screen of app, click on the schedule button
click the edit button
set min/max for the current and next day. Make sure that the settings are different
verify that the device updates to the current day settings
check the device the next day and verify that the schedule has updated to the next day settings
Use the device for 7 days. make 5 degree changes each day
from app home screen, select the history button
verify that you have history of your energy usage for the past 7 days
from a location with 2 devices installed enter the app home screen
click on the settings button
click add device
set up device 1
repeat and set up device 2
set temperature for device 1
change devices from home screen and set temperature for device 2
verify that the temperature has updated on both devices

fields.description: When modifying the temperature from a mobile device, the application delivers a server error.
fields.name: Remote Temperature Adjustment returns server error
A/C came on when 'cool' was turned to off
schedule did not update next day based on calendar settings
